decision_taken: model the term `parent` reference as a single optional parent (0 or 1), matching every farmOS consumer in this cluster (e.g. log_category's loadTree/depth assumes a tree). Drupal-core taxonomy_term `parent` is technically multi-valued (cardinality unlimited).
reversal_condition: a fixture or consumer that assigns two parents to one term and expects DAG membership/multi-path depth — at which point parent becomes a GSet-style collection and depth becomes multi-valued

decision_taken: deleteTerm removes the term from view/listings; children whose parent pointed at it are left with a dangling parent id (ancestorsOf/depthOf stop at the missing ancestor rather than re-parenting to root). No cascade or re-parent was observed in the module source.
reversal_condition: an observation of farmOS re-parenting orphaned children to root (or cascading delete) on term deletion

topic: log_category is not a pure shell — see log_category/punts.jsonl
decision_taken: four of five members (animal_type, equipment_type, lab, material_type) are confirmed pure vocabulary shells and fully spine. log_category carries a category-field-on-logs + append/replace bulk-categorize fold (big-punt w2-taxa-lc-1/lc-2) which was NOT tiered by the vocab scan; its term-store shell is built spine, the fold is punted up.
